topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The box sat on my entryway table, a splash of overly cheerful color against the muted tones of my hallway. I picked it up, the cardboard surprisingly light, and peered inside. A haphazard arrangement of bright orange lilies and some wilting purple roses. Nothing. No note. Just blooms. I let out a slow, deliberate sigh, the sound heavy in the quiet apartment. I set the box down with a soft thud, my jaw tightening as I surveyed the unsolicited display. Who thought this was a good idea? I picked up my keys, the metallic click of them sounding unnecessarily sharp. I needed coffee, strong and black, to reset my equilibrium.

[story 2]

I stared at the bouquet as if it held some hidden, offensive message. The petals seemed too vibrant, too insistent in their presence. My fingers traced the velvety texture of a particularly large rose, feeling a distinct prickle of irritation under my skin. I dropped the flowers onto the nearest flat surface—the counter—with a sharp exhale. "Seriously?" I muttered under my breath, the word tasting sour. I leaned against the kitchen island, crossing my arms tightly across my chest, watching the vibrant colors mock the dull afternoon light filtering through the window. This was excessive; I really needed a moment of peace, not this sudden, unsolicited intrusion.

[story 3]

When the delivery person finally left, I stood there, the empty box feeling like an insult. I ran a hand over my forehead, feeling the slight tension pulling at my temples. The flowers, a garish mix of pink and yellow, were just… there, demanding attention. I walked over to the living room window, staring out at the street, my shoulders slumping slightly. I picked up the arrangement again, turning it over in my hands, feeling a distinct urge to discard it. It was a gesture without context, an imposition on my space, and it left a residue of sheer exasperation clinging to the air around me. I placed it on the floor, a definitive, if silent, dismissal.
